Okay, next thing I want to talk a little bit about is some of the people, some of you guys have been asking about this. What is the plan? What's going happening? This moving forward, what's going on? So I just wanted to kind of address some of that. I'm not going to give you all the specifics. I haven't got them all finalized. We're still working on contracts with people and things, but we have some really big plans of how to take what you guys have experienced this year and make it even bigger and make it even better. One of the interesting things that I've noticed that I've heard back from some of you guys and I want to address that because one of the big fears, some of you guys are like, I am not going to get my two comical stage in February and I feel bad and I want to hide and I'm nervous and I'm embarrassed, all these things. I want to just stop you guys really quick and I don't want anyone to feel bad about that. I remember when I first got into this business, I remember when John Reese did his million dollar day and I remember having that experience and making the commitment to myself like, I'm going to make a million dollars in a year. I was like, that's all I want to do. That's when I made my decision like Myron talked about. I was like, I committed to it. I made a covenant. I'm going to do it at all costs. And I remember January was a couple months later, I started January 1st. Boom. I started tracking all my stuff and I was running as fast as I could. Fast as I could. Fast as I could. And by December of that year, I did not hit it. And I was like bummed out like, dang it. But what was cool is that January 1st was starting the next day and I was like, okay, round two, I'm starting again January 1st and the recent all clocks ready to go. And I did it again for an entire year. I got closer, closer, closer in December. I think I ended at like $907,000 and I missed it. I was like, dang it. Like, oh, January's set in a day. How can I do in a year? What's wrong with me? And luckily I stopped and had a little perspective. I'm like, I made $900,000 in a year. I was broke two years ago. And in the year number three, I went and ran and ran and ran by October. I think it was like October 17th. I hit it. So it took me three years after I set the goal. So don't feel bad if you're like, oh, I'm not like, don't stress out. That's what we're here. You guys just support you and to take you and to lead you and to guide you. And so in our job, again, this is the first year of the program and it's been great. And we're going to keep making it better and better and better. So one of the major things, the feedback, the ideas, the thoughts is we've been brainstorming as a team on 2CCX that we want to do is realize that everyone kind of came in at different levels, right? Some people are beginners and we let something happen. Some people are advanced. It was like, I never kind of got much into a group and there's been really cool synergies because of that. Where people are more advanced to help other people and vice versa. And that's been really, really cool. But as I've been thinking, like, how do we accelerate everybody faster? How do we make it better for you and simpler for you as a whole? We've said that within the 2Comical Club X program, we need a different level to be able to focus on like what you need to add a certain period of time. So what will be happening next year is basically inside of 2Comical Club, there's going to be three levels that will be tied to like where your income level is at, right? So from zero to seven figures, excuse me, zero to six, six to seven and then seven plus. Okay. At the zero to six level, you'll still be getting like all the amazing stuff you've been getting, but there's going to be very structured step by step process to get you to, to hear as fast as possible, right? Course based accountability coach is step one, step two, a very, very structured trying to get you to this point. We're bringing in somebody, I can't tell you who it is yet because I haven't finalized the contract, but somebody who's insanely amazing, who's going to become a full time audible coach, which means every single day level, open office hours, do audibles all day, every single day with all of you guys, which is, and this person is like a super star when you see, when we reveal who it is, you're going to like slip out. It's that way anytime you're stuck, you sign an audible and boom, you're in there and there's and I'll tell you who it is, an amazing human who's going to blow your freaking minds and we'll get you guys to this level, right? Along with structured course, all those kinds of things, right? As soon as you get to this point, it becomes less of like, what's my offer? What's my funnel? All the kinds of things. It's like, how do we start expanding that? Right? The interesting is I feel like at that point in your guys' journey, the thing that's the most, probably the most helpful for you, isn't always just like, like jumping on calls every single week, things like that. I feel like it's getting customized like, personalized, exactly what do you need to fix? What do you need to tweak? What do you need to change specifically, right? So when you graduate from this level to this level, we're opening up, and it's not like you're going to spend more money, but you'll come to this level and then as soon as you pass this and you say, boom, I got my six figures, then you're automatically graduate at this level. And then what happens is it opens up a mastermind group for you. And a couple of times a year, you'll come to Boise in a small group, about 35 people at a time, and you'll be sitting in a room with this same insane accountability coach, or Audable's coach, and it'll be a facilitated mastermind group, just like the ones I do for my inner circle. In fact, this person is in my inner circle. So they've been doing this with me for two years and know exactly how to facilitate them. They're very, very good. And so you'll be coming to Boise, sitting in a room, being facilitated in the mastermind experience where you're getting on stage, you're presenting your business, and they are facilitating specifically on you and your specific business. Okay. That's where you will be at as you're moving from six to seven figures. And as soon as you pass the seven-figure mark, then you will move up to the next level, which is another mastermind experience. But instead of this person coaching you, at this level, you will have, I still don't finalize this every week, but probably a couple of our coaches, probably John, Julie, Steven, and maybe James, like a couple of things like that, who will facilitate the same thing. So you come to next level, where now you've got three or four facilitators who are now helping you get from where you are to the next level. And then from there, the next level, obviously, the inner circle and the inner circle is where I'm facilitating the meetings there. And so that's kind of the levels of a sense of how we're trying to serve you guys is to get you where you're at and they'll get you at what you need. So I'm going to explain the best I can and what this concept is. It's called creating your syndicate. Okay. So for you to understand the concept of a syndicate, there's a couple of core foundational pieces you got to understand. The very first thing is you have to understand one of the problems that all of us have is we have this misguided belief that our customers are customer. Right. How many of you guys only buy stuff for me and nobody else? I love like three of you. The rest of you guys, what are you doing? You're cheating on me. This is not fair. Come on now. What else can I give you? I don't know. I'm giving you everything I got. Okay. So we have this belief that like my customer is my customer. Like I'm the only person. And so we have this like protective barriers. Like they're just my customer. Right. And so the problem, like to make this work, you have to understand, like your customer's not just your customer. Right. And so you start thinking about that. The second thing I start thinking about, and this is stuff we've talked about earlier today, you look at your, you look at your customer. Okay. And all of our customers are going through this customer journey. Right. Then you bring these people in and you explain to this concept like we're trying to build a syndicate and the way the syndicate works starts right here. Understanding like we're all serving the same customer. Right. We all want this customer to have a great experience and succeed. Right. And right now we're doing different levels. Right now you're selling in the beginner course. You're selling the next step and the next step. I'm doing the ads. I'm doing this and everyone's got these different pieces of it. It's like right now we're all serving this person doing our best. But what if we came together and figure out how can we actually serve this person in a more holistic way? Because the reality is like someone doesn't like, they don't need funnels until they figure out they don't want to be an entrepreneur. Right. And vice versa. They don't need ads. So they forgot a funnel. They don't need them. All these things are happening. So it's actually a very synergetic relationship. So everybody will promote Mike and sell the thing to serve this customer. Right. And then March will do the next person, which is like, okay, now they've got a business started. And so throughout the calendar year, everyone's got a coordinated launch where all of the biggest people in the market who all have the customer base are all focusing on serving that customer at the first step and then the second step and the third step and the fourth step and the fifth step and so on and so forth. Okay. And so that's what they built. And then the next one, the next one, next one. Okay. Right. Every two months, there was new launch. Everybody talked about it. And like you, all you saw in the marketplace was everyone talking about this person's launch and every launch they did, like on the low ended a million high end, five or $6 million every single one too much later is the next one. And there was next one. And they, between all these people's lists, like, if you look at the overlapping of everything covered up like 90% of the market. And so all you, all anyone saw was that thing. Everyone was talking about all the podcasts, the blogs, like it became this thing where like that was the conversation of the marketplace. Okay. And so if you really want to take this dream 100 strategy to the next level, you really want to dominate all the conversation in the marketplace. It's hard to do by yourself. Right. So find the partners, find the people and then building out a syndicate kind of like this. And now you've got a whole bunch of people that you're working very, very close with to serve this customer at the highest level. And that you guys is how you do the syndicate, which is the second phase of the dream 100. That good. Now, what the, I'd be really, really careful of if any of the people you pick in here happened to be like a bad person, like let's say this guy's awesome. This guy's awesome. She's awesome. But this guy actually treats customers like crap. Guess what happens to all of your shared customers? They're like, Oh my gosh, this whole thing's a scam. And that customer leaves the ecosystem for forever. Okay. So when you're doing this, it's got to be strategic. Like you have to find people who actually love their customers. You'll be shocked, but there's a lot of people in this world who do not love their customers. They just want to make money. Okay. I try to repel those people at all costs from us and from our world. Um, I try to target and interact with and coach and help people to believe in value and creating people and trading success stories and change people's lives and doing this because of the impact. Um, but there are evil people out there. Um, so I want to make sure you're aware of that because one bad apple inside of a syndicate can destroy your whole entire process. Can get people to flee your marketplace and can be really, really damaging. Right. So make sure if you decide to do something like this, you're picking these people strategically to build it out. Um, also make sure you're picking people that can, that can be part of it. Right. If, if four people don't have a list and two people do, it's not the process is going to work. Get to find people like I talked about yesterday, the levels, dream 100 people at the same level, you can coordinate together. And then we do it over and over and over again, right? Cause the biggest thing, the biggest bottleneck that we have in my company is me, right? I can't write more books, but if I can find other people in my market who've written books and I can do this piece that all of us are really good at doing, I can plug that piece in and they get them to buy ads and thank you page. It's plugging people into my webinar. How powerful is that? Right. So think about the fun ones you guys have right now and think about the other dream 100 over here who have audience that have followings, but they are really, really bad at, um, at this piece that you guys are all really, really good at. Instead of going right into the book to be able to own funnel, like use their book, build a funnel for them, give it to them, make them drive the ads. They're covering all the cost of it. All the stuff's coming through. And then the backside out, you get a customer for free. Then joins your webinar and then you make money and you start selling it. To wrap up today, I want to go back to the very first slide here, which is our framework. That's why I kind of recap this for you guys to kind of put all this in the thing. Cause obviously we've covered a lot of things in the last two days. Um, but when I was then done, like this is, this is the path. Right. And when you look at it this way, it's not super complicated. Right. I try to make it very simple. I'm a simple person. Like it comes down to some very, very basic things. Once again. Okay. Number one is you guys got to identify your dream 100. If you haven't done this exercise, like go deep into it, go figure out who those people are. Okay. They have your dream customers. They're sitting there just waiting for you to go and serve them. Number two, building a show so you can leverage your dream 100. She will help serve those people so you can use your show to be able to build relationships, to get in there and to be able to find your voice, be able to test hooks, be able to test your material. From there, you're pushing people into the traffic you control traffic. You can't control the two different angles you have from there. You push people in the front end funnels. You give them to break even from there. All the data falls down into your, into action X and your black box. And there you have follow-up funnels. Now we can start sending them up the value ladder. Now it's interesting. If you think about this, when most people talk about traffic, they only talk about the ad right now. They only talk about the ad right or the target like the basic things. But if you look at this like traffic is not really that hard. Right? If you like just traffic like anyone can log into Facebook and like put it in a thousand bucks and spend it like I spent, I bought traffic. I get there right? That's not that hard, but you have to have a business to sustain traffic. Right? If you have a business and a process for taking people through traffic goes really, really easy. If each of your customers is worth, very subscriber is worth, you know, 20 bucks, 100 bucks, a thousand bucks to you over the lifetime that customer. You can spend as much money as you want to get people back in. Right? And so it's the hardest thing when I ever talk to people about traffic is there are so frustrated, sort of traffic out of one piece. It's like, even if you got the ad to work, it doesn't matter because you're missing all the rest of the business. This is like the rest of the iceberg. When you have these things in place, traffic becomes easy. Now, obviously there's a lot of things you can do to test and tweak and make it better. But when you have the foundation, you can make a lot of mistakes. And when you have all these other things in place, it's okay. Right? I can lose my way. We have a client that I did a consulting for. They're a $50 million your company and their front end funnel is take them six months to break even. Six months. Right? But they really get on the backside of this. So their ads are inefficient. They're ineffective. They're like horrible and they lose a whole bunch of money. But six months after somebody buys the very first thing, then they break even and then after every single penny afterwards is pure profit for you guys because you're smart. You understand these things. Like you can break even immediately there to your ad spend to break even. If they can make $50 million being six months in the hole, what can you break even? I'm not seeing what's happened with our company, $0 to $100 million. Like you can, we're talking this at lunch with Steven. Like you've got two options, more and take $20 million in VC funding. Number two, just build a good front end funnel and then buy ads at it and keep tweaking it until until it converts it at a profitable rate. But that's kind of what it all is. So thank you guys so much for letting me share this with you guys and explain it to you.
